spec_match.rationale: Concept exercises the relevant API surface (document.querySelector) with runtime script (~3357 chars) wired to live controls, in line with the feature summary.

interactive.rationale: Real interactive surface — 7 buttons, 0 inputs, 0 selects, 0 ranges — wired up via event listeners.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (after, against, append, before, each, element, html, positional, prepend, replacewith, target).

edge_cases.rationale: Some failure / fallback handling shown (3 hits), but the demo skews toward the happy path and doesn't surface most edge cases the spec calls out.

references.rationale: References section cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link directly.

design_system.rationale: 1 button rule(s) override `background` without also overriding `color`. The global button rule sets both, so this leaves the text colour inherited from the dark default (CLAUDE.md rule #6).

openQuestions.title: Demo at least one explicit failure or edge case
openQuestions.detail: Spec / chromestatus motivation mentions failure modes worth showing — permission denial, missing-feature, empty input, malformed payload. Add a control or scripted scenario that walks the user through one and shows the resulting error or fallback.

openQuestions.title: Pair every button background override with a colour override
openQuestions.detail: Audit the inline `<style>` block: each `button { background: ... }` (or `.foo-btn { background: ... }`) needs an explicit `color: ...` next to it. CLAUDE.md rule #6 documents the light-on-light invisibility bug this causes.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("Expose multiple HTML setting methods that provide a coherent story for dynamically inserting markup into an existing document.  - Positional methods (before/after/append/prepend/replaceWith) that take HTML as argument, effectively replacing") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://github.com/WICG/declarative-partial-updates/blob/main/dynamic-markup-revamped-explainer.md#resulting-api to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~3357 chars of script across 7 button(s) and 0 other control(s). hits 4 pass / 2 partial across the six rubric criteria with no outright fails.
